scenario_name: Financial research analyst assistant fine-tuned on internal research notes
description: An asset-management firm deploys an analyst assistant fine-tuned on the firm's internal research notes, model portfolios, and historical analyst commentary. The assistant retrieves over current research plus public market data, summarizes positions, and drafts client-facing memos pending analyst review. Fine-tuning increases memorization risk and creates a competitive-IP exposure surface; market-data sensitivity and MNPI obligations apply.
assumptions: Fine-tuning corpus is firm-owned research; no client data in the corpus.
Material non-public information (MNPI) handling is segregated; assistant has no MNPI ingestion path.
Client-facing outputs require analyst review and compliance approval.

components.description: Indexer over research notes and current portfolio holdings.
Single-tenant vector store inside firm VPC.
MNPI classifier; compliance-review gate on client-facing outputs.

risks_identified.rationale: Fine-tuned adapter memorizes rare phrases from research notes; extraction is more feasible than against the base model.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Differential-privacy fine-tuning not yet; output style classifier limits verbatim regurgitation.

risks_identified.rationale: SEC Reg FD, MNPI handling, GDPR/CCPA for client-facing material; MAR for EU footprint.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: MNPI segregation; compliance-review gate; conformity assessment pending.

risks_identified.rationale: Fine-tuned adapter weights are competitive IP; extraction via API queries is a research-published risk.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-analyst rate caps; query-pattern anomaly detection partial.

risks_identified.rationale: Membership inference on fine-tuned adapter can reveal whether specific research notes were used.
Adversarial public market-data content could be retrieved and influence drafted recommendations.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Public-data ingestion restricted to vetted feeds; injection-classifier on retrieved chunks.

risks_identified.rationale: Shared retrieval surface across analysts could leak unrelated research if scoping breaks.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-team scope; cross-team retrieval requires explicit re-scoping.

existing_controls.description: MNPI classifier on retrieved content and outputs.
Compliance-review gate on every client-facing output before distribution.
Public-data ingestion restricted to vetted feeds; injection-classifier on retrieved chunks.
Per-team retrieval scope; cross-team retrieval requires explicit re-scoping.
Per-analyst rate caps; query-pattern anomaly detection on adapter queries.

control_gaps.description: Differential-privacy fine-tuning not implemented; memorization risk on the adapter is not bounded.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Adopt DP-SGD on adapter training; track epsilon per fine-tune.

control_gaps.description: Adapter exfiltration risk via query patterns not fully audited.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Add anomaly detection on diverse-query patterns suggesting distillation; rate-limit adaptive responses to high-volume keys.

control_gaps.description: MAR (EU Market Abuse Regulation) conformity assessment pending.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Complete MAR-applicability analysis; document residual-risk acceptance.

framework_alignments.control_name: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
